DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-14-2024 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearance via Zoom:
Morgan Chase, Esq., Tel. 619.237.5200, for defendant Jackson Hewitt, Inc.;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT JACKSON HEWITT INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The matter is not contested. No argument is held. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Jackson Hewitt Inc.'s demurrer to pro per plaintiff Timothy Martin's third amended complaint ("TAC") is sustained without leave to amend.

Martin was fired from his job as a tax preparer for H&R Block for not registering with the California Tax Education Council ("CTEC"). (TAC Ex. A-3.) Martin maintains he is exempt from this requirement because he is a trustee of "THE FRIENDS OF FAIRYDUSTMARTIN" charity. (Id. Ex. A-8.) Martin sued various public and private entities including Jackson Hewitt, an alleged representative on CTEC's board. Martin claims Jackson Hewitt negligently failed to understand the Tax Preparation Act (Bus. & Prof. Code 22250 et seq.) in breach of its fiduciary duty as a CTEC board member, causing Martin to lose his job. Jackson Hewitt now demurs to the TAC on multiple grounds.

Jackson Hewitt argues that Martin fails to state sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action against Jackson Hewitt for negligence. (CCP 430.10(e).) A negligence claim requires the plaintiff to show "(1) the defendant owed the plaintiff a legal duty, (2) the defendant breached the duty, and (3) the breach proximately or legally caused (4) the plaintiff's damages or injuries." (Thomas v. Stenberg (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 654, 662.) On the first element, Martin argues that Jackson Hewitt "had a legal duty to understand the exemption section of the 'Taxpayer Act' and to make sure that Businesses were registered." (Opp. 4:7-9.) Martin has not alleged a cognizable legal duty that Jackson Hewitt owed to him, and thus he fails to state a claim for negligence.

To the extent Martin also asserts a breach of charitable trust cause of action (the TAC mentions it in passing), this too fails. Corp. Code 5142(a) allows someone "with a reversionary, contractual, or property interest in the assets subject to such charitable trust" to sue for breach of charitable trust. While Martin alleges he is the trustee of the "THE FRIENDS OF FAIRYDUSTMARTIN" charitable trust, he does not allege a reversionary, contractual, or property interest in the trust. Nor does Martin allege how Jackson Hewitt damaged any such interest. Martin fails to state a claim for brief of charitable trust. (CCP 430.10(e).)

Leave to amend a complaint may be denied after several failed attempts to state a cause of action. (Stevenson v. San Francisco Housing Authority (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 269, 284.) It is the plaintiff's burden to establish how the complaint can be amended to state a valid cause of action. (Sanowicz v. Bacal (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 1027, 1044.) Here, Martin has not demonstrated how he can amend the complaint to fix its deficiencies. Further, the complaint remains defective despite several opportunities to amend. Accordingly, the demurer is sustained without leave to amend.

The Court to review the proposed order submitted by the prevailing party.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Jaime. Matter not reported.

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ATTORNEY GENERAL FOR THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

Before the court are six demurrers, from defendants California Tax Education Council (CTEC), California Society of Tax Consultants (CSTC), HRB Green Resources LLC (HRB), California Society of Enrolled Agents and National Association of Enrolled Agents (CSEA/NAEA), California Franchise Tax Board (FTB), and the Attorney General for the State of California. The demurrers are sustained without leave to amend.

In Pro Per Plaintiff Timothy James Martin filed a second amended complaint (SAC) alleging various causes of action against multiple defendants, including wrongful termination, retaliation, deceptive practices, statutory violations, breach of trust, negligence, and more. The SAC and accompanying declarations indicate that Martin worked for H&R Block Inc. until February 13, 2023, when he was terminated for failing to register with defendant CTEC, a non-profit corporation that registers tax preparers under California law. (SAC at p. 8-13.) Martin alleges that he was exempt from registration as a trustee of a charitable trust. (SAC at p. 8-9.) However, the FTB examined Martin's claimed exemption and concluded that he was not exempt from registration with CTEC. (SAC at p. 9-11.) While the crux of Martin's claims in the SAC center around CTEC and a non-party, H&R Block Tax Services, Inc., Martin has also attached FTB, HRB Green Resources LLC, the Attorney General, CSTC, and CSEA/NAEA as parties to the suit.

"A demurrer tests the legal sufficiency of factual allegations in a complaint." (Rakestraw v. California Physicians' Serv. (2000) 81 Cal. App. 4th 39, 42-43.) To withstand a demurrer, a plaintiff must show that the complaint alleges facts sufficient to establish every element of each cause of action. (Id. at p. 43.) If any element of a cause of action is negated, a demurrer to the cause of action is properly sustained. (Cantu v. Resolution Trust Corp. (1992) 4 Cal. App. 4th 857, 880.) If a complaint fails to state a cause of action, the burden is on the plaintiff to demonstrate the manner in which his complaint can be amended. (Hendy v. Losse (1991) 54 Cal. 3d 723, 742; Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal. 3d 311, 318.) Additionally, a demurrer is also proper when the allegations in the complaint are uncertain, ambiguous, or unintelligible. (CCP 430.10(f).)

Martin's SAC is uncertain, ambiguous, and unintelligible, and fails to state a cause of action as to all demurring defendants. Martin does not mention defendant CSTC in his SAC and does not allege any activity by defendants CSEA/NAEA or HRB Green Resources LLC (perhaps confusing defendant with non-party, H&R Block Tax Services, Inc.). As to the Attorney General, Martin alleges in his SAC that he has named OAG as a defendant in the case because he has a "duty to inform" the Attorney General of conduct of other defendants but does not claim that OAG has done anything that may give rise to a cause of action. (SAC.) As to the FTB and CTEC, Martin's allegations appear to center around the FTB's opinion that he was not exempt from the Tax Preparer Act's registration requirements with the CTEC. (Id.) Again, the SAC does not assert any legal duty or facts that would provide a cause of action against the FTB or CTEC.

Martin does not allege any facts supporting any of the causes of action against any defendant.

The court sustains the demurrers. Additionally, because it is clear that Martin could not amend the complaint to state a cause of action against any defendant, the demurrers are sustained without leave to amend.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
